brief_summary: Two labelled vertical housing-recipe stacks side by side. Left: all-nylon top and bottom (Durock T1, Gazzew Boba U4T) with a low, muted sound-wave glyph. Right: polycarbonate top over nylon bottom (Gateron Lanes, Drop Holy Panda X) with a taller, brighter sound-wave glyph. Grounds the article's claim that the T1's housing recipe is a minority choice among 2026's flagship tactiles and explains the acoustic consequence of that choice.
data_sources: durock-t1-deep-dive.mdx § 'Housing: all-nylon, and what that buys acoustically' — 'Both housing halves are nylon, and the effect is a switch that mutes itself end to end ... the same housing recipe the U4T uses'
